reasoning: While programmatic signals indicate high text retention (98.7% recall), the LLM fidelity report identifies significant structural failures. Specifically, the corruption of the 'Reporting Points of Contact' table grid and the injection of page header logos into the middle of a multi-page list severely disrupt the reading order and accessibility. Given the cost asymmetry of 3x for bad documents, these major structural defects require manual correction.

concerns.analysis: The table 'Reporting Points of Contact' (Index 25) contains several structural errors. First, the column headers ('State Agencies' and 'Local Governments...') in the second row (Index 25, Row 1) are marked as 'data' cells rather than 'header' cells. Second, and more critically, the final row (Row 4) contains only a single cell. Because the table is established as having two columns in preceding rows, a row with only one cell and no 'colspan' property corrupts the table grid, making the content a
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The table on page 2 has two distinct columns. The final row has content in the left column ('Contact the Enterprise Security...') and an empty space in the right column.
concerns.output_quote: In the table at element index 25, row 4 (is_header_row: false) contains only one cell object: 'Contact the Enterprise Security and Risk Management Office at mailto:DITThreatManagement@nc.gov.'
concerns.location: How to Report Cyber Incidents to the State, Page 2, element index 25

concerns.analysis: The reading order is disrupted by the injection of page header images into the middle of a list. In the source, a list regarding cyber incident criteria starts at the bottom of page 1 and concludes at the top of page 2. The logos (header) for page 2 appear at the very top of the page. However, the output places these images (Index 13, 14, 15) between the first part of the list (Index 12) and the second part (Index 16). This forces a user to read through irrelevant header/logo metadata in the mid
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The list of incident criteria begins on Page 1 ('Result in a significant loss...') and continues seamlessly onto Page 2 ('Indicate unauthorized access...'). Logos are in the header area.
concerns.output_quote: The images (Logos) at indices 13, 14, and 15 are positioned in the reading order between list items at index 12 and index 16.
concerns.location: When to Report to the State, between Page 1 and Page 2

concerns.analysis: The visual organization of the document is significantly disrupted. The header logos (NCDIT, DPS, and the Great Seal) which appear at the very top of the source PDF have been moved and inserted into the middle of the document content. Specifically, they are placed between the second and third bullet points of the list under the 'When to Report to the State' section. This breaks the logical flow of the list and the overall document structure, making it feel disjointed and misorganized.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The source PDF has logos at the top of Page 1. A continuous bulleted list begins on Page 1 ('Result in a significant loss of data...') and continues on Page 2 ('Indicate unauthorized access to...').
concerns.output_quote: In the HTML, the three logos (DPS, NCDIT, Great Seal) are inserted between the list item 'Have an impact on a large number of victims' and the list item 'Indicate unauthorized access to...'.
concerns.location: In the 'When to Report to the State' section, between the second and third bullet points.

concerns.analysis: A single continuous list in the source has been split into two separate list objects (Index 12 and Index 16) due to the intervention of the page header content. This changes the meaning of the document, as the list at Index 16 appears as a new, unrelated list rather than a continuation of the requirements specified in the paragraph at Index 11.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: A single list of 5 bullets spanning the page break between page 1 and page 2.
concerns.output_quote: Two separate list elements at index 12 (2 items) and index 16 (3 items).
concerns.location: When to Report to the State, Page 1 and 2

action_items: Repair the 'Reporting Points of Contact' table by ensuring proper header cell tags and applying correct colspan to the final row.
Relocate or remove the injected page 2 header logos (Indices 13, 14, 15) so they do not interrupt the list content.
Re-join the split list items at Indices 12 and 16 into a single semantic list.
